top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic TD Five Boro Bike Tour's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
TD Five Boro Bike Tour's flat paved city streets and bridges forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 40 miles.
When someone attacks on flat paved city streets and bridges, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
40 miles on flat paved city streets and bridges burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
TD Five Boro Bike Tour's flat paved city streets and bridges rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
2-minute all-out efforts on flat paved city streets and bridges. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At TD Five Boro Bike Tour, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
At TD Five Boro Bike Tour, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of TD Five Boro Bike Tour. Train it until it feels boring.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what TD Five Boro Bike Tour's flat paved city streets and bridges actually does to you.
Base fitness for TD Five Boro Bike Tour. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Varied gradients on flat paved city streets and bridges demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for TD Five Boro Bike Tour’s 40 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, Gravel Specific, and VO2max to prepare you for 40 miles of flat paved city streets and bridges in New York City, New York.
